title: Spatial multi-omic map of human breast tissue
summary: Builds a spatial reference of the human breast integrating transcriptomics, proteomics, and imaging across normal and tumor-adjacent tissue, resolving epithelial, stromal, and immune niches.

title: Cell-cell communication inference from spatial transcriptomics
summary: Proposes a graph-based method to infer ligand-receptor signaling between neighboring cells in spatial data, benchmarked on breast and lung tumor sections.

title: The immune microenvironment of triple-negative breast cancer
summary: Characterizes immune cell states and their spatial organization in triple-negative breast cancer, linking T-cell exclusion patterns to response to checkpoint blockade.

title: Squidpy: a scalable framework for spatial omics analysis
summary: Introduces a Python toolkit for storing, analyzing, and visualizing spatial single-cell data, including neighborhood enrichment and spatial statistics used widely in tumor microenvironment studies.

title: Fibroblast niches shape drug response in breast tumors
summary: Uses spatial transcriptomics and organoid co-cultures to show that cancer-associated fibroblast niches modulate chemotherapy response in breast tumors, nominating stromal targets.
